compiled_state.title: [debug-everything Phase B Step 2]: grep -c on single-line JSON array -> OPEN_BUG_COUNT always returns 1 instead of actual count
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
### 2. Incident Overview

* **Scenario Type:** User-Flagged Behavior

#### Expected Behavior

Phase B Step 2 of /dso:debug-everything counts all open+in_progress bug tickets and enters Bug-Fix Mode when OPEN_BUG_COUNT > 0. The count should equal the number of ticket objects returned.

#### Actual Behavior

The count command uses \`grep -c '"ticket_id"'\` on the output of \`ticket list\`. When the CLI returns a compact single-line JSON array (all tickets on one line), \`grep -c\` returns 1 (line count) regardless of how many tickets exist. In this session, 22 tickets were present but the initial count returned OPEN=1, IN_PROGRESS=1, TOTAL=2.

The skill then only sees 2 bugs, potentially skipping Bug-Fix Mode entirely if the threshold logic depends on the exact count for routing decisions.

#### Reproduction

1. Run \`.claude/scripts/dso ticket list --type=bug --status=open | grep -c '"ticket_id"'\` when 20+ bugs exist.
2. Observe output is 1 (one line with JSON array), not 20+.

#### Root Cause

\`grep -c\` counts matching *lines*, not matching *occurrences*. Compact JSON arrays emit all data on a single line, so grep -c always returns 1 when there are results.

#### Fix

Replace \`grep -c '"ticket_id"'\` with \`grep -o '"ticket_id"' | wc -l\` (counts occurrences, not lines) in SKILL.md Phase B Step 2.

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral. Fix: replaced grep -c with grep -o | wc -l in Phase B Step 2 to count occurrences instead of lines.
comments.author: Test
